testcase.name: ARA-S-1 ARA-S-2 provider-neutral contract reports quota wait before start
ARA-S-2 waiting runtime dispatch re-probes after capability recovery
ARA-S-2 auth denial remains a typed resumable stop
PRC-S-3 typed provider failure survives status normalization
ARA-S-3 successful implementation result is structured and HEAD-bearing
ARA-S-4 review requires separate identity and closed parallel provenance
ARA-S-4 review requires review capability before provider start
ARA-S-4 review rejects a provider result that impersonates the implementer
ARA-S-4 review rejects stale HEAD and same implementation session
ARA-S-4 review result must correlate to the reviewer runtime returned by start
ARA-S-4 review start without session or thread provenance is contained
ARA-S-4 review dispatch identity changes with the implementation session
ARA-S-4 review runtime is read-only at request and result boundaries
ARA-S-4 review rejects a mutable provider sandbox before start
ARA-S-4 review validates started identity and contains the wrong runtime
ARA-S-2 permission wait during polling becomes a typed Run stop
ARA-S-2 permission wait clears stale Run stop after provider resumes
ARA-S-5 adapter id participates in dispatch identity
ARA-S-6 provider operations enforce the requested timeout
ARA-S-5 duplicate dispatch is reused and cancel confirms terminal runtime
ARA-S-5 nonterminal cancel fails closed as orphaned agent
ARA-S-5 cancel escalates to force before declaring an orphan
ARA-S-6 cancel provider failure escalates and reports an orphan when containment fails
ARA-S-5 start timeout invokes dispatch-scoped force containment
ARA-S-5 start timeout is orphaned unless dispatch-scoped containment confirms terminal status
ARA-S-5 generic start failure is contained before redispatch can occur
ARA-S-6 timeout and malformed success never become completion
ARA-S-5 operation timeout contains the provider runtime before allowing redispatch
ARA-S-5 operation timeout reports an orphan when containment cannot confirm terminal state
ARA-S-5 result timeout contains the provider runtime before failing
ARA-S-7 adapter definition rejects incomplete provider contracts
PRC-S-1 Codex connector conforms to probe/start/status/result/cancel
PRC-S-2 Claude Code is explicit opt-in and shares the connector contract
PRC-S-3 probe failures normalize auth, quota, permission and unavailable states
PRC-S-3 Codex probe requires explicit authenticated output
PRC-S-4 review uses read-only sandbox, separate identity and a separate provider session
PRC-S-3 cancellation confirms a terminal provider status
PRC-S-3 requested timeout terminates the provider and remains typed
PRC-S-3 malformed provider output fails closed at result collection
PRC-S-3 provider quota failure remains typed after process start
PRC-S-3 terminated permission failures are terminal and remain typed
PRC-S-7 production composition preserves an explicitly injected coordinator
PRC-S-6 production smoke probe is typed and never requires credentials
